original: Victor Steele is one of the richest men in the galaxy. His fortune was gained through years of adventuring during the Thirteenth Great Planet Rush, almost two hundred years ago. His trillion dollar company, Steele Industrial Technologies and Production, or Steele Tech, is one of the most respected names in its field.
translation: 维克托·斯蒂尔是银河系中最富有的人之一。他的财富是在大约两百年前的第十三次大行星拓荒时期通过冒险获得的。他万亿美元的公司——斯蒂尔技术与生产，或斯蒂尔科技，是这个领域最受尊敬的名字之一。

original: Unfortunately, during his adventures, Victor underwent many mutations that wreaked havoc on his genome. After two centuries of life due to longevity treatments, the mining magnate’s doctors have told him he has a measly two decades left.
translation: 不幸的是，在冒险期间，维克多经历了许多变异，对基因组造成了严重破坏。在两个世纪的长寿治疗后，这位矿业巨头的医生们告诉他他还剩下二十年的时间。

original: The news is unwelcome. Still, he’s lived a rich, full life - fuller than most. With twenty years ahead of him, the suddenly old industrialite looks toward siring a successor; an heir or heiress to carry on his legacy and to ensure that the Steele name is honored throughout all of history.
translation: 这个消息并不让人高兴。不过，他过着富裕，充实的生活——比大多数人都充实。这位突然间变老的工业家需要一个接班人来继承他的遗产，并确保斯蒂尔这个姓氏会名垂千古。

original: With the next Planet Rush a scant 23 years ahead, the timing is ideal. Just as his offspring reaches adulthood, the United Galactic Confederacy will be expanding its borders, turning on the hundreds of warp gates it has shot out into unexplored space. The rushes - performed every century or so - always result in a galactic free-for-all.
translation: 距离下一次星际拓荒还有短短的23年，这个时机恰到好处。正当他的后代长大成人时，银河统一邦联将会扩大边界，开启它射向未开发过的太空发射的数百道折跃门。每个世纪，银河系都会因此向拓荒者们自由开放。

original: It’s perfect, Victor thinks. His child could rise to greatness, becoming richer and more powerful than he ever was. And perhaps, with a bit of luck, a better person as well.
translation: 维克多认为，这很完美。他的孩子可能会成出人头地，变得比他都更富有更强大。或许，运气好点的话，还能成为一个更好的人。

original: The real question is, who should the mother be? Many lovers spring to mind, both human and alien. After all, he has no shortage of them. Should his offspring be a pure human, like himself, or perhaps something a little more exotic? Decisions, decisions...
translation: 真正的问题是，母亲应该是什么样的人？无数的恋人涌入脑海，不论是人类还是外星人。毕竟，他并不缺乏这些恋人。他的后代应该像自己一样是个纯粹的人类，还是更富有异国情调的物种？再三决策……

original: What species would you like your character to be?
translation: 你希望你的角色是什么物种？

original: Victor’s child will be born a full-blooded human.
translation: 维克多的孩子生下来，会是纯血人类。

original: Victor’s child will have a dog-like ausar for a mother. Half-ausars will come into the world with anubis-like ears, canine genitalia (if male), additional hair color choices, more eye color choices, and a long, fluffy tail.
translation: 维克多的孩子会有一个像狗一样的奥萨尔人母亲。半奥萨尔人来到世上时，会长着阿努比斯般的耳朵，若是男儿身，则生有犬类的生殖器，此外还有更多的发色与瞳色可选，以及一条蓬松的长尾。

original: Victor’s child would have a gryvain mother. The gryvain are a technologically advanced race of winged hermaphrodites: a halfbreed child would have scaled legs and human-style arms, a fleshy upper body, a pair of draconic wings, and a lengthy tail. Gryvain also have monochrome gold eyes and frilled ears which their children inherit, though most children with human fathers lack horns. Halfbreed gryvain can be born female or as hermaphrodites, and both sexes tend to have voluptuous figures and high sexual sensitivity.
translation: 维克多的孩子会有一个格里温人母亲。格里温人是科技发达的带翼雌雄同体种族：混血儿会拥有覆鳞的双腿、人类式的手臂、肉质的上半身、一对龙翼和一条长尾。格里温人还有单色的金瞳和褶边耳朵，这些特征会遗传给后代，不过大多数父亲是人类的混血儿没有角。半格里温人出生时可能是女性，也可能是雌雄同体，无论哪种性别，往往都体态丰满，性敏感度极高。

original: Victor’s child will have a kaithrit mother, famed for their feline resemblance and doubled tails. Half-kaithrit come into the world with two feline tails, cat ears, additional hair color choices, more eye color choices, and cat genitalia (if male).
translation: 维克多的孩子会有一个凯斯里特人母亲，这个种族以酷似猫科的外貌和双尾而闻名。半凯斯里特人降生时，会带着两条猫尾、一对猫耳，发色和瞳色的选择也更多。如果是男孩，还会长着猫的生殖器。

original: Victor’s child would have a kui-tan mother, a race known for its total lack of females and similarities to earth raccoons. Of course, that would make the mother a hermaphrodite - a woman with a vagina and a penis. Half kui-tan usually come into the world as a male or hermaphrodite with one bushy tail, fuzzy ears, and a knotty dick.
translation: 维克多的孩子会有一个猫狸人母亲——这个种族完全没有女性，外形与地球上的浣熊相似。当然，这意味着母亲会是雌雄同体：一个同时拥有阴道和阴茎的女人。半猫狸人降生时，通常是男性或雌雄同体，带着一条毛茸茸的尾巴、一对毛茸茸的耳朵，还有一根带节的鸡巴。

original: Victor’s child would have a leithan mother, though that race’s unique biology would mandate some very expensive scientific intervention to ensure a successful pregnancy. Leithans are powerfully built, six-legged reptile-taurs. Half-leithans come into the world with thick, prehensile tails; unique bunny-like ears; and a tauric body configuration. They have limited skin and hair color options compared to other races. Half-leithan males are born with large reptilian genitalia, and both sexes have rear-mounted sexual organs.
translation: 维克多的孩子将有一个雷坦人母亲，不过雷坦族独特的生物学特性需要一些非常昂贵的科学干预才能确保成功怀孕。雷坦人是一种体格健壮的六足爬行类人。半雷坦人来到这个世界时，拥有粗大的前伸尾巴、独特的兔子耳朵和半身人的身体构造。与其他种族相比，他们的皮肤和头发颜色选择有限。半雷坦人的雄性天生就有大型爬行动物的生殖器，雌雄都有后置的性器官.

original: Victor’s child would have a suula mother. Suula are a race of large, winged aliens with a distinctly shark-like appearance. A halfbreed child would have a partially-scaled body, feathery hair, and a powerful shark’s tail. Suula are a colorful race and, regardless of sex, tend to be fairly feminine and voluptuous in figure. Half-suula can be male or female.
translation: 维克多的孩子会有一个苏拉人母亲。苏拉人是一种体型庞大、长着翅膀的外星人，有着明显类似鲨鱼的外形。混血会有部分被鳞片覆盖的身体、羽毛构成的头发和强有力的鲨鱼尾巴。苏拉人是一个色彩斑斓的种族，无论性别如何，身材都相当女性化和丰满。半苏拉可以是男性，也可以是女性。

original: A purely human heritage would give Victor’s child the body one would expect - two legs, two arms, two eyes, a head of hair, etc.... There’s really not much more to say about such a choice.
translation: 纯粹的人类遗产会给维克多的孩子带来人们所期望的身体——两条腿、两只胳膊、两只眼睛、一头头发，等等……对于这样的选择，真的没什么好说的了。

original: As a half-ausar, Victor’s child would start with dog-like ears, a canine tail, and a red, knotted penis if male. The ausar are known for their close bond with humanity and would be a likely pick for the child’s mother.
translation: 作为一个半奥萨尔人，维克多的孩子一开始就会有狗一样的耳朵、狗一样的尾巴，如果是男性，还会有一个红色的、锁结的阴茎。奥萨尔人以与人类的亲密关系而闻名，她们很可能会成为孩子的母亲。

original: The kaithrit are a cat-like race with two prehensile, feline tails. They are known for their feminine appearances and exotic colorations, so any child of Victor and kaithrit would have more possible hair colors, a prettier face than normal, and two tails. Also, if the child is a male, it’ll have a soft-spined, cat-like penis.
translation: 凯特里特人是一个像猫一样的种族，有两条伸缩自如的猫尾。他们以女性化的外表和奇异的肤色而闻名，因此维克多和凯特里特人的孩子会有更多可能的发色、比正常人更漂亮的脸蛋和两条尾巴。此外，如果孩子是男性，还会有一个软刺、像猫一样的阴茎。

original: Leithans are a race visually similar to mythological centaurs, though they trace their origins to reptile-like species and have six clawed legs. They have powerful, prehensile tails as well as a highly acute set of four ears: two tapered ones on the side of their heads, and two large bunny-like ears atop. They are known for their speed and strength, and have a distinct color palette of grays and blacks, with yellow bioluminate areas on their scales. Leithans are also much taller than normal, reaching natural heights up to nine feet tall. If the child is male, it will have a large, bulbous reptilian penis between its rear legs.
translation: 雷坦人在外观上与神话中的半人马相似，但他们的起源类似于爬行动物，并且有六只爪子。他们有强有力的前伸式尾巴，以及高度锐利的四只耳朵：两只锥形耳朵长在头侧，两只兔子般的大耳朵长在头顶。它们以速度和力量著称，体色以灰色和黑色为主，鳞片上有黄色的生物发光区。雷坦人也比常人高得多，自然身高可达 9 英尺。如果孩子是男性，它的后腿之间会有一个巨大的球状爬行动物阴茎。

original: The kui-tan are a raccoon-like race who invariably have a phallus with multiple knots. They are also known to have fuzzy, rounded ears, fluffy tails bigger than many species’ adolescent young, and balls that engorge with seed the longer they go without release.
translation: 猫狸人是一种类似浣熊的种族，它们的阴茎上总是有多个结，还长着毛茸茸的圆耳朵，毛茸茸的尾巴，比许多物种的幼崽都要大，睾丸越长越大，越长越多。

original: Gryvain are a highly advanced race of winged hermaphrodites, blending mammalian and reptilian biology. They have darkly scaled limbs, fleshy upper bodies, prehensile tails, curling horns, and frilled ears. Their eyes are dark gold and vertically slitted. All gryvain are feminine in appearance, and most have voluptuous figures: broad egg-laying hips and large breasts. They have bulbous, reptilian phalluses with internal testes, mounted over vaginas that have rings of internal, nub-like secondary clitorises inside, which makes birth and sex extraordinarily pleasurable.
translation: 格里温人是一种高度发达的有翼雌雄同体种族，融合了哺乳动物和爬行动物的生物学特性。它们的四肢有深色的鳞片，上身肉质，尾巴前伸，长有卷曲的角和流苏状的耳朵。它们的眼睛是暗金色的，呈垂直狭缝状。所有蝼蛄的外表都很女性化，大多数都有着丰满的身材：宽大的产卵臀部和硕大的乳房。它们的阴茎呈爬行动物的球状，内部有睾丸，安装在阴道上，阴道内部有环状的、像阴核一样的次级阴蒂，这使得生产和性爱异常愉悦。

original: The suula are large, predatory humanoid sharks. They’re considered a race of beautiful amazons, standing taller and more powerfully-built than humans, with sharp teeth and muscular wings and tails. They have shark-like scales over their body, colorfully-feathered wings and arms, and muzzles full of sharp teeth. Though anyone can see the average suula is quite voluptuous, more intimate encounters reveal that their sexual organs are full of venomous stingers that impart powerful aphrodisiacs to themselves and their partners.\n\nSuula-human hybrids typically favor their human parent, appearing as amazonian individuals with feathery hair and smoothly-scaled forelimbs. Males tend to be smaller than females, but quite well endowed.
translation: 苏拉人是一种大型掠食性人形鲨鱼。她们被视为美丽的阿玛宗种族，比人类更高大强壮，长着锋利的牙齿、肌肉发达的翅膀和尾巴。她们全身覆盖着鲨鱼般的鳞片，翅膀和手臂上长着色彩斑斓的羽毛，嘴里满是利齿。虽然谁都能看出普通苏拉人身材相当丰满，但更亲密的接触会揭示，她们的性器官布满毒刺，能给自己和伴侣注入强效催情素。\n\n苏拉与人类的混血儿通常更像人类亲本，外貌是阿玛宗式的个体，头发如羽毛般轻盈，前肢覆着光滑的鳞片。雄性往往比雌性体型小，但天赋异禀。

translation: 这对鸳鸯一心只想给未来的孩子最好的，于是前往维克多的私人诊所，急切地要确保他未来的继承人只继承最优秀的基因。路上，这位矿业巨头牵着孩子母亲的手，两人脸上都挂着心照不宣的笑容。

original: When they arrive, Victor’s doctor sits him down, while his chosen wife is taken to the medbay. He tucks his holo-glasses into his coat pocket, looking at the expectant father with a warm smile.
translation: 当他们到达时，维克多的医生让他坐下，而他的妻子则被送往医疗室。他把全息眼镜塞进衣兜里，面带微笑地看着这位准爸爸。

translation: 也许最好的办法就是适度，什么都给一点时间。什么都不偏爱，就意味着他的孩子不会在任何方面有特长，但这也意味着[pc.heShe]也不会缺少任何东西。

original: live in the lap of luxury. Things are quite easy for you, but at the same time, you feel the warmth of being provided for by your trillionaire father.
translation: 过着锦衣玉食的生活，万事不愁。但与此同时，你也能感受到身为万亿富翁的父亲供养你的那份温情。

original: ’re gently encouraged to take part in physical activity. Taking to it like a duck to water, you spend much of your youth sweating and putting your body to work.
translation: 被鼓励参加体育锻炼。就像鸭子喝水一样，你的大部分青春时光都在挥洒汗水和锻炼身体。

original: ’re gently encouraged to take up more mental pursuits. Taking to it like a duck to water, you spend most of your time reading or expanding your thinking. Your test scores are the envy of your academy.
translation: 被鼓励去追求更多的精神生活。就像鸭子喝水一样，你把大部分时间都花在阅读或拓展思维上。你们的考试成绩让学校羡慕不已。

original: don’t experience the luxuries one might expect from the heir to Steele Tech. Just like your father, you work for everything you have. Forged from adversity, you learn to value what you’ve earned, rather than expecting it to be given to you for free.
translation: 作为斯蒂尔科技公司的继承人，生活并不奢华。就像你父亲一样，你的一切都要靠自己努力。在逆境中磨练，你学会珍惜自己的所得，而不是指望别人白白给你。

original: ’re always trying a bit of everything and anything. In the end, you come out quite balanced for it, just as your father had hoped you would.
translation: 总是什么都试，什么都试。最后，你会因此而得到平衡。正如你父亲所希望的那样。

original: live your life as an enigma. There is nothing particularly worth mentioning about your early upbringing in life whatsoever...
translation: 的一生是一个谜。你早年的生活经历没有什么特别值得一提的......

original: Still, the one thing a parent can never choose is exactly what kind of person their children will be. When your friends talk about you, what word do they use to describe your personality?
translation: 不过，父母永远无法选择的一点是，他们的孩子究竟会成为什么样的人。当你的朋友谈论你时，他们会用什么词来形容你的个性？

original: You let out a long sigh. Now that you’ve finished your schooling, your whole life is ahead of you. Memories of your dad pushing you forward spring to mind. After all, you’ve got a whole mega-corp to take over one day.
translation: 你长长地叹了口气。如今学业已了，整个人生就在眼前。父亲当年鞭策你向前的记忆涌上心头。毕竟，偌大的企业帝国，终有一天要交到你手上。

original: Still, before you take over the reins, it seems he wants you to take up a profession. He’s got some suggestions - your dad always does - and they’re pretty odd ones at that.
translation: 不过，在你接手之前，他似乎想让你先找个营生。他提了些建议——你爸总是这样——而且都是些稀奇古怪的建议。

original: You stare at the list. Smuggler, Mercenary, Tech Specialist... and Kineticist? As intriguing as these choices are, are they really okay job prospects for a future CEO?
translation: 你盯着名单。走私者、雇佣兵、技术专家……还有心能师？这些选项虽然有趣，可对未来的CEO来说，真算得上正经出路吗？

original: What kind of class would you like your character to be?
translation: 你希望自己的角色属于哪一类？

original: As a smuggler, your abilities would rely heavily on having good reflexes and either aim or physique, depending on your method of attack. You’d learn to be pretty sneaky, fly well, and hit your foes where they least expect it, all while making your living in the underbelly of the United Galactic Confederacy, or U.G.C. The life of a smuggler is one of profit through luck and skill.
translation: 作为一名走私者，你的本事主要靠反应快，再搭配精准的枪法或过人的体格，具体看你怎么出手。你得学会神出鬼没，飞得一手好船，专挑敌人最意想不到的地方下手，同时窝在银河联合邦联的灰色地带讨生活。走私者这行，靠的是运气和本事吃饭。

original: As a mercenary, you’d depend on raw physique or aim for your attacks, focusing more on a good battle plan and tough armor than anything else. You’d learn to overpower your foes with sheer strength and determination, defend yourself with all manner of weapons and equipment, and fly a ship when the situation calls for it. The life of a mercenary is one of profit through violence.
translation: 作为一名雇佣兵，你只能依靠原始的体格或瞄准来进行攻击，而更多的是注重良好的作战计划和坚硬的盔甲。你要学会用纯粹的力量和决心制服敌人，用各种武器和装备保护自己，并在必要时驾驶飞船。雇佣兵的生活就是通过暴力来获取利益。

original: As a tech specialist, your abilities would rely heavily on your intelligence and aim. You’d learn to work with all kinds of technologies, perhaps even make your own robotic defense turrets! Your intellect would be your greatest weapon, though you wouldn’t shy away from tweaking your own high-tech ranged weaponry. The life of a tech specialist is one of profit through smart decisions and superior technology.
translation: 作为一名技术专家，你的能力在很大程度上取决于你的智力和瞄准能力。你要学会使用各种技术，甚至可以自己制造机器人防御炮塔！智力将是你最强大的武器，不过你也不会回避调整自己的高科技远程武器。技术专家的生活就是通过明智的决策和卓越的技术来获利。

original: As a kineticist, you would receive a brain implant to augment your latent telekinetic abilities, allowing you to rely almost completely upon your own willpower and intelligence. You’d learn to rely on activated special abilities instead of basic melee or ranged attacks, though maintaining a basic aptitude in one or the other wouldn’t hurt - at least until your mental powers fully blossom. The life of a kineticist is one where strength of mind is greater than any strength of body.
translation: 身为心能师，你将接受脑部植入，以强化你潜在的心灵致动能力，让你几乎可以完全仰仗自身的意志与智力。你将学会依赖激活的特殊能力，而非基础的近战或远程攻击，不过，在两者之中保持一项基本能力也无妨——至少在你的精神力量完全绽放之前是这样。心能师的人生，便是心灵之力远胜于任何肉体力量的人生。

original: Is this the career you’d like to pursue?
translation: 这是你想从事的职业吗？

translation: （你的角色已完成装备！因此，你的最大意志和淫性将根据装备的决意和性感度而波动。这是正常现象，关于各种战斗属性及其相互影响的更多信息，请参阅你的智典。）

translation: 你是想体验故事和教程，还是跳过？

original: Your dad’s health had been declining throughout your years of education, but you never really worried too much about it. He was super rich, able to afford his own private doctors, so the thought that one day he could disappear never entered your mind. When you started your career, he was there to see you off, smiling gregariously, but your job made it difficult to stay in touch. Most communications were done via written messages, until even those became scarce. You had assumed it to be a consequence of your different schedules and busy work lives, then...
translation: 在你求学的那些年里，父亲的身体每况愈下，但你从没太放在心上。他那么有钱，养得起私人医生，你压根儿没想过有一天他会离开。你刚入行的时候，他还笑呵呵地来送过你，可工作一忙，联系就难了。起初还靠书信往来，后来连信也稀了。你总以为是两人都忙、时间凑不上，直到……

original: The news of his death arrived in a brief missive, containing an invitation to a funeral as well as a travel voucher to get you there. You quit your job on the spot. The way Dad talked, you’re sure he would have wanted you to take over the family business. What could have killed him? He always seemed so big and imposing. Even when his back was bent by age, he still had vital energy about him, seeming as busy as a man a tenth of his age. You don’t know why anti-agathic treatments stopped working on him. Worse, he aged far more rapidly than he should have, losing his youth in a short decade.
translation: 他的死讯随着一封短笺传来，里面附了葬礼的请柬和一张送你去的旅券。你当场辞了职。听爸爸从前说话的口风，你确信他希望你接手家业。是什么害死了他？他看起来总是那么高大威严。即便被岁月压弯了脊背，他身上仍有一股活力，忙得像个年纪只有他十分之一的人。你不知道抗衰老疗法为什么对他失效了。更糟的是，他衰老的速度远超正常，短短十年就耗尽了青春。

original: You shake your head to chase away the unwelcome thoughts and focus on packing. The trip is boring, leaving you with nothing to think about except life without your father and the uncertain future ahead. Hopefully the old man was lucky enough to go out in a dignified way.
translation: 你摇摇头，赶走这些恼人的念头，专心收拾行李。旅途乏味，脑子里翻来覆去，无非是没有父亲的日子该怎么过，前路又该如何走。但愿老人家运气不坏，走得还算体面。

original: Your transport pulls up to Tavros Station, where the service is being held. Adjusting your finest clothes, you walk through the airlock and up some old-fashioned steps towards the theater Dad’s money bought him, trying to keep your eyes clear as you breeze past a surprisingly low amount of paparazzi. They must have gotten decent security this time.
translation: 你的交通工具停靠在塔夫罗斯空间站，葬礼就在这里举行。你整了整身上最体面的衣服，穿过气闸，踏上古旧的台阶，朝那座剧院走去——那是爸爸用钱给他买下的。你快步掠过狗仔队，人数少得令人意外，你努力让目光保持清澈。看来这次安保做得不错。

original: You push open the doors and step inside...

The funeral is closed casket with no viewing, which is expected given the shape Dad was in the last time you saw him. His body was riddled with the lines of age and marked with liver spots and pre-cancerous lumps, and he didn’t want to be remembered that way. Thankfully, the ceremony is swift and fairly small. There are only family and friends here, though you wager a large amount of the ‘family’ is distant relations hoping to catch some of the inheritance.
translation: 葬礼是闭棺的，没有瞻仰遗容，这也在意料之中，毕竟你最后一次见到爸爸时，他的身体状况很糟。他身上布满岁月的沟壑，长着肝斑和癌前肿块，他不愿别人记住他这副模样。好在仪式简短，规模也小。在场的只有家人和朋友，不过你估计，这所谓的“家人”里，大半都是想来分一杯羹的远亲。

original: Much of the wake passes in a daze, with you swarmed by distant relations and well wishers, forced to shake hands and share hugs with people you don’t know while they offer barely-felt, useless platitudes that do nothing to assuage your loss.
translation: 守灵的大部分时间，你都浑浑噩噩。远亲与吊客蜂拥而至，将你团团围住。你不得不与陌生人握手、拥抱，听他们说着不痛不痒的废话，丝毫不能抚平你的丧亲之痛。

original: You’re on your way over to your father’s real friends, people you’ve actually seen around his mansion growing up, when his lawyer intercepts you and pulls you aside.
translation: 你正要去找你父亲真正的朋友，那些你在他的豪宅里见过的人。这时他的律师拦住了你，把你拉到一边。

original: You follow the lawyer’s brisk stride with a bit of difficulty. He’s a samarin; long-limbed, yellow-skinned, and not overly inclined to slow his walk for your comfort. He also talks the entire way.
translation: 你跟在律师轻快的步伐后面，有点困难。他是个萨马林人；四肢修长，黄皮肤，不会为了让你舒服而放慢脚步。他还一路不停地说话。

original: “Your father’s will was a bit unconventional. He’s lucky my firm has had such wonderful dealings with him over the years, or I would not have been willing to undertake such a... complicated executorship.”
translation: “你父亲的遗嘱有点不合常规。他很幸运，我的公司多年来一直与他保持着良好的合作关系，否则我也不会愿意承担如此......复杂的遗嘱执行人职责。”

original: You shrug, figuring your dad had a lot of wealth to distribute.
translation: 你耸耸肩，认为你父亲有很多财富要分配。

original: The lawyer’s quills bristle at your noncommittal response, but he carries on. “Regardless of the headache my best client’s death has caused me, I’m pleased to officiate this bit of business. Victor spoke rather highly of you, and he didn’t say that about just anyone, I assure you.”
translation: 律师的鹅毛笔被你模棱两可的回应激得微微发颤，但他还是继续说了下去。“尽管我这位最好的客户的离世给我带来了不小的麻烦，我仍很高兴能主持这桩事务。维克多对你评价颇高，我向你保证，他可不是对谁都这么说的。”

original: The lawyer turns to regard you with a smile as he waits for you to catch up, clapping you on the back when you’re in reach. “My name is Maki. We’ve never been formally introduced, I suppose.” He extends a coarse, dry hand and you shake it
translation: 律师转过身，含笑看着你，等你走近。你一到跟前，他便拍了拍你的背。“我叫马基。想来，我们还没正式认识过。”他伸出一只粗糙干涩的手，你握了上去。

original: , commenting that he already has your name.

He continues, “To be brief, your dad rented out most of the hangars on this station for you. He also had a state of the art, xeno-compatible daycare facility built here to house any future offspring you create. I have to give him credit, he planned ahead. Construction has been going on here for the past four years at his behest.”
translation: 他继续说：“简单地说，你爸爸为你租下了这个空间站的大部分机库。他还在这里建了一个最先进的、与异种兼容的托儿所，用来安置你们未来的后代。我不得不称赞他的未雨绸缪。在他的要求下，过去四年来这里一直在施工。”

original: Grinning at your surprise, the tawny alien opens a door to the side and steps inside, still talking. “Victor knew he’d be dying around this time since before your birth. I suppose he didn’t want the knowledge weighing over you. Medical science is amazing, but your father underwent so many mutations in his life that he was barely considered human by the time he finished carving out his fortune. Sure, he looked fine when he came home, but he only managed that by taking on the most humanesque mutations from the various races he encountered. I shudder to imagine how long that took him, or how expensive it was to purchase specific splices.”
translation: 面对你的惊讶，这位黄褐肤色的外星人咧嘴一笑，打开旁边一扇门走了进去，嘴里还在说个不停。“维克多在你出生前就知道自己会在这时候死。我想他是不愿让你背负这份沉重。医学确实神奇，但你父亲这辈子经历了太多变异，等他终于挣下这份家业时，已经很难算作人类了。当然，他回家时看着挺好，但那全靠他从各个种族身上挑最像人的变异来用。光是想想这花了多少时间，买那些特定拼接体又花了多少钱，我就不寒而栗。”

original: Well, that explains the melancholy tone some of his missives took whenever you had been making plans to visit him in the future. A plain metal chair creaks as the lawyer takes his seat behind a similarly austere desk, smoothing his quills into a semblance of slicked-back hair. He opens a drawer and drops two items on the table: a simple injector and a rectangular, leather-wrapped object. The injector is filled with a silvery, viscous fluid. The other object has the leather wrapped around one side of it, leaving polished metal sides exposed.
translation: 难怪你每次计划去看他，他的信里总透着一股忧郁。律师在一张同样简朴的桌子后坐下，那把普通的金属椅子吱嘎作响。他捋了捋身上的刺，权当是向后梳起的头发。他打开抽屉，拿出两样东西放在桌上：一支普通的注射器，还有一个长方形、裹着皮革的物件。注射器里装着银色的粘稠液体。那物件只裹了一面皮革，其余几面露出抛光的金属。

original: “Take them, they’re yours. Both custom work. Both very, very powerful pieces of technology.”
translation: “拿去吧，归你了。两件都是定制的，都是威力极强的科技造物。”

original: You heft the tablet, wondering how it could be that expensive, even with the Earth-leather binding and quality materials used in its construction. Once you pull back the cover, you expose a high resolution tablet running a custom operating system.
translation: 你掂了掂这台平板，心里犯嘀咕：就算用了地球皮革装帧，用料也讲究，也不至于贵到这个地步吧。翻开盖子，一台运行着定制系统的高清平板便露了出来。

original: “Greetings, "+{var0}+". I am the Codex. You may address me as ‘Codex’. I am a simple virtual intelligence, or V.I. in common parlance, programmed to assist you in cataloging new life, resources, and technologies. I am equipped with long-range extranet transmitters and receivers, allowing for communication and data access in most locations. There is a short-range life sensor embedded in me that allows me to warn you of impending danger. Upon detecting a new species, I will collate all extranet-available data on the species for you to peruse within a minute or two. I have haptic feedback on my screen and do accept voice commands, "+{var1}.mf("Master","Mistress")+" "+{var2}+".”
translation: “你好，"+{var0}+"。我是智典。你可以称呼我为‘智典’。我是一个简单的虚拟智能体，通俗地讲就是 V.I.，我的程序可以帮助你对新生命、新资源和新技术进行编目。我配备了远程外联网发射器和接收器，可以在大多数地方进行通信和数据访问。我身上还装有一个短程生命传感器，可以在危险来临时向你发出警告。探测到新物种后，我会在一两分钟内整理出该物种的所有外联网可用数据，供你浏览。我的屏幕上有触觉反馈，也接受语音指令，"+{var1}.mf("主人","主人")+" "+{var2}+"。”
context: <<POS:18120077>> <<VARS:var0=pc.short,var1=pc,var2=pc.short>> output("\n\n“Greetings, "+pc.short+". I am the Codex. You may address me as ‘Codex’. I am a simple virtual intelligence, or V.I. in common parlance, programmed to assist you in cataloging new life, resources, and technologies. I am equipped with long-range extranet transmitters and receivers, allowing for communication and data access in most locations. There is a short-range life sensor embedded in me that allows me to warn you of impending danger. Upon detecting a new species, I will collate all extranet-available data on the species for you to peruse within a minute or two. I have haptic feedback on my screen and do accept voice commands, "+pc.mf("Master","Mistress")+" "+pc.short+".”")

original: You fold the sophisticated computer’s cover closed and find that its supple case comes complete with a connector that allows it to be easily hung from a belt. Snapping it on, you look closer at the injector and ask, “So what’s this then?”
translation: 你合上这台精密电脑的盖子，发现它柔软的外壳上配有一个连接器，可以方便地挂在腰带上。你把它扣好，凑近细看那支注射器，问道：“那这又是什么？”

original: The lawyer steeples his fingers and says, “Microsurgeon immune boosters. Top of the line. Your dad will tell you more.” He gestures towards a second door set into the wall beside you. “Go on through.”
translation: 律师十指交叉，说道：“微观免疫强化术械。顶尖货色。你父亲会跟你细说。”他朝你身旁墙上的第二扇门一摆手。“进去吧。”

original: You take the injector and open the door, unsure just how much weirder this can get...
translation: 你拿着注射器，打开门，不知道这还能有多古怪......

original: The next room is empty, save for a comfy looking, leather recliner. You actually recognize it as one of your father’s favorites. The arm rests are only a little more worn than you remember. A holo projector protrudes from the ceiling, aimed at the empty space in front of the chair. With nothing else to do, you seat yourself. The projector kicks on and your dad’s face appears.
translation: 隔壁房间空荡荡的，只有一张看起来很舒适的真皮躺椅。你认出来了，这是父亲最爱坐的一张。扶手比记忆中磨得更旧了些。天花板上伸出一台全息投影仪，正对着椅子前的空地。左右无事，你便坐了下来。投影仪亮起，父亲的面容浮现眼前。

original: “Hey there, "+{var0}.mf("sport","princess")+". If you’re seeing this, then my wrinkled old ass has finally kicked the bucket. I hope you aren’t too torn up over it; I’ve lived a long full life, longer than most people are lucky enough to live. Do me a favor and knock back a whiskey for me after this,” the phantom image of your father says. A hand clips in front of his face as he rubs at his age-silvered goatee. “Unfortunately, while I am seeing that your mother is taken care of, I’m not just handing the company over to you. Not yet, anyway.”
translation: “嘿，{var0}。如果你看到这个，那么我这个满脸皱纹的老登终于一命呜呼了。我希望你不要太伤心，我已经活了很久，比大多数人的寿命都要长。帮我个忙，喝完这杯酒后给我倒杯威士忌。”你父亲的幻影说。他用一只手在自己的脸前比划着，一边揉搓着他那岁月沧桑的山羊胡子。“不幸的是，虽然我看到你母亲得到了照顾，但我不会就这样把公司交给你。反正现在还不行。”
context: <<POS:18121960>> <<VARS:var0=pc>> output("\n\n“Hey there, "+pc.mf("sport","princess")+". If you’re seeing this, then my wrinkled old ass has finally kicked the bucket. I hope you aren’t too torn up over it; I’ve lived a long full life, longer than most people are lucky enough to live. Do me a favor and knock back a whiskey for me after this,” the phantom image of your father says. A hand clips in front of his face as he rubs at his age-silvered goatee. “Unfortunately, while I am seeing that your mother is taken care of, I’m not just handing the company over to you. Not yet, anyway.”")

original: You glance down at the Codex and the vial in your hand. What did the old coot plan for you?
translation: 你低头瞥了一眼手中的智典和小瓶。这老东西到底给你安排了什么？

original: “I know you’ve probably been too busy to keep up on the news, with the work I’ve pushed you into, but the fourteenth Planet Rush ought to be starting about now.” A note appears over the recording, indicating the Planet Rush started almost two months ago, though most gates have only started going online in the past few weeks. “I have to make sure you’ve grown into a "+{var0}.mf("man","woman")+" worthy of running the business, "+{var1}+", so I’m putting you through what I went through, after a fashion. If you want to take over the company, you’re going on the Planet Rush!”
translation: “我知道，我逼你做的那些工作，大概让你忙得没空看新闻。不过，第十四次大淘星潮应该马上就要开始了。” 录音上浮现出一行注释，指出大淘星潮差不多两个月前就开始了，不过大多数星门是最近几周才陆续开启的。 “我得确保你已成长为一名配得上经营这份事业的"+{var0}.mf("男人","女人")+"，"+{var1}+"，所以，我打算让你也尝尝我当年的滋味，算是依样画葫芦吧。想接手公司，你就得去参加大淘星潮！”
context: <<POS:18122638>> <<VARS:var0=pc,var1=pc.short>> output("\n\n“I know you’ve probably been too busy to keep up on the news, with the work I’ve pushed you into, but the fourteenth Planet Rush ought to be starting about now.” A note appears over the recording, indicating the Planet Rush started almost two months ago, though most gates have only started going online in the past few weeks. “I have to make sure you’ve grown into a "+pc.mf("man","woman")+" worthy of running the business, "+pc.short+", so I’m putting you through what I went through, after a fashion. If you want to take over the company, you’re going on the Planet Rush!”")

original: Huh. Well, you suppose the careers he pushed you towards make a bit more sense in light of that. Your father made his fortune during the thirteenth Planet Rush, and he obviously intends for you to prove your mettle in the same way.
translation: 嗯。这么一想，他逼你走的路，倒也有了几分道理。你父亲是在第十三次大淘星潮中发家的，显然，他也想让你用同样的方式证明自己。

original: “Maki should have given you the Codex and immune boosters by now. I’m sure the V.I. inside it has already explained what it can do for you, but to summarize: it’s going to be your best friend. You can use it to stake claims on untapped minerals and resources that the boys back home will run through the legal system. We’ll deliver you a stipend on each usable discovery.”
translation: “马基现在应该已经把智典和免疫增强剂交给你了。我相信里面的虚拟智能已经跟你解释过它能做什么，但简单来说：它会是你最好的伙伴。你可以用它来圈定未开发的矿产和资源，后方的人会走法律程序帮你落实产权。每发现一处可用的资源，我们都会给你一笔津贴。”

original: Victor coughs repeatedly, holding a rag up in front of himself that darkens with what you assume to be flecks of blood in the monochromatic recording. You’d think he could have sprung for a color hologram. His coughs clear up and he continues, “Sorry, health isn’t what it used to be.” He laughs after that, realizing that for you, he’s dead. “Anyhow, the immune boosters. Those micro-scale bots will reproduce in your body and safeguard it from some of the worst the universe can throw at you. They aren’t perfect, and more benign infections might slip through, but they’ll keep you from catching rot lung from a Trinerian. A word of caution: they’ll help you digest things that would normally be impossible for a human, but they’ll splice you to do it. You eat the wrong native foods, and you’ll wind up looking like a native. You might even start to feel like one, mentally. I’m told it’s like forced, convergent evolution. Point is, it isn’t perfect. Relying on it too often will build up too much Taint in your system, and you’re better off avoiding that.” Victor’s features fall at the mention. “Read up on it in your Codex if you haven’t already. I don’t want you winding up like me. You’re a good kid, and if you play your cards right, you’ll get to live three times as long.” His eyes are watery. “Take care, kiddo.”
translation: 维克多反复咳嗽着，拿着一块抹布挡在自己面前，在单色的录像中，抹布上的血迹逐渐变深。你觉得他应该用彩色全息图的。他的咳嗽声消失了，继续说道：“对不起，健康状况不如从前了。”说完他笑了，意识到对你来说，他已经死了。“总之，免疫增强剂。这些微型机器人会在你的身体里繁殖，保护你免受宇宙中最恶劣的环境的伤害。它们并不完美，更多的良性感染可能会漏网，但它们会让你远离特里尼人的肺瘟。需要注意的是：它们会帮你消化一些人类通常无法消化的东西，但它们会给你做接合。如果你错吃了本地食物，你就会看起来像本地人。你甚至会开始在心理上觉得自己是当地土著。我听说这就像是被迫的、趋同的进化。关键是，它并不完美。过于频繁地依赖它，会在你的系统中积累过多的污染，你最好避免这样做。”提到这个，维克多的五官都垮了下来。“如果你还没读过的话，就在你的法典中读一下吧。我不希望你落得和我一样的下场。你是个好孩子，如果你的牌打得好，你会有三倍的寿命。”他的眼睛水汪汪的。“保重，孩子。”

original: You turn the microsurgeon immune boosters over in your hand, debating whether to use them.
translation: 你把微观免疫强化剂在手里翻来覆去，犹豫着要不要用。

original: “This recording is set up to repeat until you do it, "+{var0}.mf("son",pc.short)+".”
translation: “这段录音会一直重复，直到你照做为止，"+{var0}.mf("儿子",pc.short)+"。”
context: <<POS:18125362>> <<VARS:var0=pc>> output("\n\n“This recording is set up to repeat until you do it, "+pc.mf("son",pc.short)+".”")

original: Sighing, you press the injector port to the inside of your arm.
translation: 叹口气，你把注射器按在手臂内侧。

original: “This recording is set up to rep-”
The injector hisses as it pricks your skin, pouring its payload into your veins. There’s a bit of burning pain, but nothing as bad
translation: 注射器嘶嘶作响，刺入皮肤，将药液推进血管。灼痛袭来，但不算什么。

original: as the time you dropped your cargo on your foot, breaking more than a few toes in the process
translation: 顿时你把它们掉在脚上，摔断了几个脚趾头

original: as the time you took a slug in the leg
as the time you shocked yourself on an arc spanner
translation: 就像你被弧形扳手电击一样

original: as the time you lost control over your powers and superheated the air around your finger while trying to pull off a party trick
translation: 就像你在派对上耍把戏时失去了对超能力的控制，把手指周围的空气都加热了

original: as the time you picked up a bad case of spooking cough while on vacation in the Vania system
translation: 就像你在瓦尼亚星系度假时得了严重的咳嗽

original: . You grow a little flushed for a moment as the tiny machines settle into you. It passes swiftly. All things considered, you feel... the same.
translation: 。当这些小机器在你身上安家落户时，你的脸瞬间变得有些潮红。但很快就过去了。从各方面来看，你感觉......没啥变化。

original: “Atta "+{var0}.mf("boy","girl")+". I had something like that made for me after a particularly rough infection, though it wound up mutating me from exotic species’ sexual fluids as well.”
translation: “好"+{var0}.mf("小子","姑娘")+"。在一次特别严重的感染之后，我得到了一个类似的东西，不过它也让我从外来物种的性液中发生了变异。”
context: <<POS:18126511>> <<VARS:var0=pc>> output("\n\n“Atta "+pc.mf("boy","girl")+". I had something like that made for me after a particularly rough infection, though it wound up mutating me from exotic species’ sexual fluids as well.”")

original: A fucking horndog until the end. Of course.
translation: 直到最后都是个该死的色鬼，肯定的。

original: The recording smiles wistfully for a moment before resuming, “I suppose that might have contributed to my demise, but damn, it was worth it. You’ll see for yourself once you get out there.”
translation: 录像俏皮地笑了一会儿，然后继续说道：“我想这可能是我死的原因之一，但该死的，这一切都是值得的。等你到了那里，你自己就会明白。”

original: “I’ve arranged to have a few dozen probes sent through the gates once they open. Each one is military grade, hardened and keyed to only open for one of our family, so don’t lose too much of yourself. They all have clues pointing towards other probes secreted inside them, except for one. One has my personal, encrypted key. With that, you can open my deposit box in the bank I own on this station and take control of my company.” Even projected through the soulless holoprojector, your dad’s spirit shines through his tear-misted eyes. “I’m sure you’ll make me proud out there.”
translation: “我已经安排好了，等大门一开，就送几十个探测器过去。每一个都是军用级别，坚固无比，而且只有我们家族的人才能打开，所以你不用太担心。它们内部都藏着指向其他探测器的线索，只有一个例外。其中一个存着我的私人加密钥匙。有了它，你就能打开我在这个空间站银行的保险箱，接管我的公司。”即使是通过冰冷的全息投影仪投射出来，你父亲的精神依然透过他泪雾朦胧的眼睛闪耀着。“我相信你在外面会让我骄傲的。”

original: The projection of your father tilts its head, gesturing towards another door behind it. “Through the door behind, you’ll find a hangar with the ship I started my journey on, repaired to usable condition and outfitted with standard, modernized technology. It’s a good starting point, but you should see if you can jump up to something better, particularly if you pick up the number of crewmates I did. That little junker can only hold a few people.”
translation: 你父亲的投影歪了歪头，朝身后的另一扇门示意。“穿过这扇门，你会看到一个机库，里面停着我当年启程时用的那艘船。已经修好了，能飞，还配上了标准的现代化设备。拿来起步倒是不错，不过你最好还是看看能不能换艘更好的，尤其是等你像我当年那样，招到那么多船员之后。那艘小破船可塞不下几个人。”

original: You rise to progress, but Dad keeps talking. “One more thing... there’s a Galotian in the next room, right next to the ship’s key. If you want to do this, you’ve gotta subdue her. Galotians are voracious protein hunters, but they’re not truly dangerous. She’s still a tough little foe, though. I’ve set up some recordings to assist you in learning how to handle creatures like this during your adventures.” Dad winks. “Good luck!”
translation: 你起身要走，爸爸却还在说。“还有件事……隔壁房间有个伽洛蒂亚人，就在飞船钥匙旁边。想干这事，你得先制服她。伽洛蒂亚人贪吃蛋白质，倒不算真危险，不过这小东西可不好对付。我录了些东西，能帮你们在冒险时学会怎么应付这类生物。” 爸爸眨眨眼。“祝你好运！”

original: You open the doorway and step through a little hesitantly, your hands on your "+{var0}+" and "+{var1}+" in case you need them. Visible light slowly increases as the systems dial up in response to your presence, illuminating an amorphous green blob that huddles in the corner. The semi-transparent, emerald mass bulges out at the base and turns to regard you. translation: 赛莉斯欢快地抖动着，腰部以下积聚的粘液随着她的掌声激动地翻腾着。她大口大口地呼吸着空气，滑稽地膨胀起来，然后小声地吐出“Yayyyy......”你好奇地看了她一眼，她害羞地捂住嘴，低声说：“对不起。”

original: Whatever, it’s time to get this show on the road! You grab the digikey off the shelf and step through the door.
translation: 不管了，该上路了！你从架子上抓起数码钥匙，迈步出门。

original: A sprawling, brightly lit hangar greets your eyes, empty save for a single ship near the back. Your gaze is pulled away from the distant craft by the sheer size of the enclosure. A hangar like this must have cost your father a veritable fortune to buy out - not much considering how wealthy he was, but no insignificant chunk of change. Magnetically-bounded plasma shields hold in the atmosphere while remaining transparent enough for you to view the nearby warp gate and inky blackness beyond. Perhaps the only thing this place couldn’t hold would be a capital ship, but a number of moorings with connective umbilicals stand by to tether one outside in that extreme scenario.
translation: 一片宽敞明亮的机库映入眼帘，除了后方孤零零停着一艘飞船，这里空无一物。你的目光从那艘远处的飞船上移开，被这巨大空间的规模所震撼。像这样的机库，你父亲买下来肯定花了一大笔钱——以他的财富来说不算什么，但也不是一笔小数目。磁约束等离子护盾将大气牢牢锁住，同时又足够透明，让你能望见附近的折跃门和门外无边的黑暗。这地方唯一容纳不下的，大概只有主力舰了。不过，真要遇到那种极端情况，旁边还备着不少带连接脐带的系泊装置，可以把船拴在外面。

original: You chuckle and wonder if you’ll ever need anything that grandiose while you turn your attention back to the light craft in the corner, currently surrounded by techs. Breezing up to them, you get your first good look at your inheritance; in short, it’s a bit of a junker. The hull plating is ancient and rusted in a dozen places where the paint has long since rotted off. Whole pieces of it are still on the floor, being cut into with plasma torches to replace dead tech, and the design of it is a century out of date. You bite back your sigh before it leaves your mouth, noting the newly installed engines and computer systems. Still, new engines don’t make it look any less like an elderly rim-jockey’s heap.
translation: 你轻笑一声，心想自己大概永远用不上那么浮夸的东西。目光转回角落那艘轻型飞船，它正被一群技师围着。你快步上前，总算看清了这份遗产的真面目——说白了，就是一堆破铜烂铁。外壳装甲老旧不堪，油漆早已剥落，露出十几处锈迹。整块整块的船板还摊在地上，技师们正用等离子割炬切割，好替换掉报废的零件。这船的设计，更是落后了整整一个世纪。你瞥见新装的引擎和计算机系统，硬是把涌到嘴边的叹息咽了回去。不过，就算换了新引擎，它看起来也还是一副老掉牙的破落户模样。

original: “Hello there! You must be the young Captain Steele! A pleasure to meet you,” one of the technicians says once he spots you. “Sorry the ship is in such rough shape. Towing it all the way out here took a little longer than anticipated, so we got a late start. We’re pulling a double shift to get it all done by tonight. Just give us a little more time, maybe get a bite to eat and a place to stay till morning. She’ll look a hundred times better once we finish up, I promise.”
translation: “你好！你一定就是年轻的斯蒂尔上尉了！很高兴见到您。”其中一位技术人员在发现你后说道。“很抱歉这艘船的状况如此糟糕。把它拖到这里来花的时间比预计的要长一点，所以我们起步晚了。我们现在是两班倒，今晚就能全部完工。再给我们一点时间，也许我们可以吃点东西，找个地方住到天亮。我保证，我们完工后，她会比现在好看一百倍。”

original: You shake the hangar tech’s hand and nod to him, understanding the enormity of the project before them. The nametag on his jumper lists him as ‘Zeke’. He nods and jovially quips, “Yah, my name’s Zeke, or ‘Z’ if you really wanna get short with it. Whatever you bring in here, I’ll keep maintained or fix up. Cleaning up other folks’ messes is kinda my business.” Zeke waves you off. “Go on, get something to eat. I’m sure you had a shitty day and don’t wanna spend the rest of it hanging around us while we put this back together.”
translation: 你握住机库技术员的手，向他点点头，明白了他们面前项目的艰巨性。他跳伞上的名牌上写着“泽凯”。他点点头，风趣地打趣道：“是啊，我叫泽凯，如果你真的想简短一点，也可以叫我“小泽”。无论你带来什么，我都会继续维护或修理。收拾别人的烂摊子是我的事。”齐克挥手让你走开。“去吧，吃点东西。我相信你今天一定过得很糟糕，不想在我们把这一切恢复原状的时候，还在我们身边闲逛。”

original: A clearly marked exit portal dilates as you approach, and you step out into the station’s main thoroughfare. It’s surprisingly quiet and devoid of the shops you’d expect to see. The only place with any grub looks to be an “Anon’s Bar”, though it boasts mention of rooms for rent as well.
translation: 当你走近时，一个标识清晰的出口门户会放大，然后你就会走到车站的主干道上。这里出奇的安静，没有你期望看到的商店。唯一有食物的地方似乎是一家“无名酒吧”，不过它也提到有房间出租。

original: Heady alcoholic scents combine with drug-laced smoke and salty food to give this place the unquestionable aroma of a seedy bar. Three-breasted waitresses balancing trays full of drinks one-handed sashay around in short skirts and sheer tops, their gait made awkward by their three legs and small feet. One of them notices you and asks, “A drink for you, hun?”
translation: 酒气、迷幻烟雾和咸食的味道混在一起，让这地方透着一股不折不扣的下流酒吧气息。三乳的女招待穿着短裙和薄透上衣，单手托着满盘酒水，扭着腰肢走来走去。三条腿和小脚让她们的步态有些别扭。其中一个注意到了你，问道：“来一杯吗，亲爱的？”

original: After a moment, you decide to order a whiskey. Regardless of your personal feelings, Dad asked you to knock one back for him. You may as well humor the dead codger, even if he did make acquiring your inheritance a trial and a half. At that point, your belly rumbles, and you realize that you haven’t had anything to eat since this morning.
translation: 过了一会儿，你决定点一杯威士忌。不管你自己怎么想，爸爸让你替他喝一杯。就当是哄哄那个死老头子开心吧，虽然他让你继承遗产这事，折腾得够呛。就在这时，你肚子咕噜噜叫了起来，这才想起从早上到现在，你还粒米未进。

original: “How about I bring out some hot buns with that for you to munch on. You can look over the menu while you snack.” She doesn’t wait around for an answer, moving off to drop a single drink off at a nearby table on her way to the kitchen. The drink is scooped up by a figure obscured by the bar’s dusky atmosphere. It’s difficult to make out much about... the humanoid shape, but its gender and features elude you. There’s nothing else to do, so you ponder the nearby person... just what sex are they?
translation: “要不，我拿几个热乎的小面包给你垫垫肚子。你边吃边看菜单。” 她没等你回答，转身就走，顺路往旁边桌上搁了杯酒，便进了厨房。酒吧里光线昏暗，只见一个人影伸手接过那杯酒。你眯起眼，却怎么也看不清……那轮廓倒是人形，可究竟是男是女，长什么样，一概分辨不出。反正闲着也是闲着，你便打量起近旁这个人来……这家伙，到底是男是女？

I just wanted to let you know that I left not long after you went to bed. My ship does have luxurious sleeping quarters for ten, after all. Ta ta!”
translation: 当你站起来时，智典发出哔哔声，并提示“收到信息”。你打开信息想读一读，却被你那下作堂亲的声音打断了，"+{var0}.mf("他","她")+"说，“早上好，瞌睡虫。我只是想让你知道，我在你上床睡觉后不久就离开了。毕竟，我的飞船上有十个人的豪华睡房。拜拜！”

translation: “信息完毕，”智典轻描淡写地宣告。操！

original: You hastily don your gear and scramble over to the hangar, hoping it has a replicator capable of making something approximating a decent breakfast. The techs are gone, probably sleeping after a full night’s work. The ship looks completely different all finished up. Its black-and-gold paint job looks as good as new, shining in the dim running lights. Looking at it again, you realize that you recognize this from some of your father’s old holo-pics: the same ship that he took out on the Thirteenth Planet Rush almost two centuries ago!
translation: 你匆忙套上装备，跌跌撞撞冲向机库，指望那儿有台复制机能弄出顿像样的早饭。技师们都不在，多半忙了一宿，这会儿正补觉。完工的飞船焕然一新，黑金涂装在昏暗的航行灯下闪着光，跟刚出厂似的。再一细看，你心头一震——你在父亲那些老全息照片里见过它：近两百年前，他就是开着这艘船，奔赴第十三次大淘星潮！

original: (Ship Acquired: Casstech Z14)\n\nAfter [rival.name]’s head start, there’s no time to waste! You pull up the first set of coordinates - to a planet known as Mhen’ga, and blow through the undocking procedures as fast as the local authorities will allow.
translation: (获得飞船：卡斯泰克Z14)[rival.name]已经抢先一步，不能再浪费时间了！你调出第一组坐标——目的地是姆恩加，然后以当地政府允许的最快速度，一口气冲完了离港程序。

original: You can soon feel the light drive throbbing through the deck, sending your little Casstech hurtling towards the shiny new Warp Gate - the connection point between Tavros and the newly-contacted Rush Worlds, unexplored and unexploited until just a few months ago when the gates of the Fourteenth Rush first came online. According to the reports you can find on the galactic extranet, Mhen’ga should be a lushly-forested world full of new life, and newly civilized by the first wave of settlers who have-
translation: 你很快就能感觉到光速推进器在甲板下震颤，推着你那艘小小的卡斯泰克，朝那座崭新的折跃门疾驰而去。这道门连接着塔夫罗斯和新近接触的拓荒世界——那些世界无人探索，也无人开发，直到几个月前，第十四次淘星潮的星门才首次上线。根据你在银河外联网上查到的报告，姆恩加应该是个森林茂密、生机勃勃的世界，第一批定居者已在那里初建文明，他们——

original: The Casstech shudders violently, and you’re lurched forward with sudden concussive force from behind. The safety harness on the captain’s chair is the only thing that keeps you from cracking your head open on the control panel. What the hell is going on!? All of your instruments go wild, and warning lights flash from every console. Fire suppression gel squirts onto the deck behind you as smoke starts rising from somewhere down in the engine crawlspace.
translation: 卡斯泰克号剧烈地颤抖起来，你被突然从后面传来的冲击力推向前方。只有船长椅上的安全带才让你的头没有撞到控制面板上。这到底是怎么回事？所有的仪器都疯狂地运转起来，每个控制台都闪烁着警示灯。你身后的甲板上喷出了灭火凝胶，引擎舱内的某处开始冒烟。

original: You grab the fire extinguisher from under your chair and kick the panel open, spraying it down before things can get out of hand. Luckily the fire is small, just a smoulder from one of your engine components... but that’s still some part of your engine that’s fried.
translation: 你从椅子下面抄起灭火器，一脚踹开面板，趁火势还没蔓延赶紧喷了下去。万幸火不大，只是发动机某个零件在冒烟……但这毕竟意味着发动机有部件烧坏了。

original: Oh shit, that’s the plasma coagulation conduit - one of the most expensive parts of your light drive’s internal workings... that’s not going to be easy to find a replacement for, especially given how old this little cutter is.
translation: 哦，该死，那是等离子体凝聚导管——光速推进器内部最贵的部件之一……想找个替换件可不容易，尤其这艘小切割船都这么老了。

original: Looks like a plasma tube of some kind fried itself! Damn it, that’s going to be expensive to fix...
translation: 看来是某个等离子管烧坏了！真该死，修起来肯定要花不少钱……

original: More importantly, the thrum of the engines has fallen off completely. Somehow, you just know this is [rival.name]’s doing. [rival.HeShe] must have snuck aboard after the techs all finished up for the night and cut the plasma tube open. And now you’re drifting in open space, with nothing but thrusters left online.
translation: 更要命的是，引擎的嗡鸣彻底消失了。不知怎的，你就是知道，这是[rival.name]干的好事。准是等技师们夜里收工后，[rival.HeShe]偷偷溜上了船，切断了等离子管。现在可好，你漂在空旷的太空里，除了推进器，什么都没了。

original: Obviously, that’s bad. Real bad. You could drift back to Tavros, but that would set you back by weeks at thruster speeds, and the cost of the repairs is far more than the pittance you’ve still got saved up from your previous job. There has to be something you can do!
translation: 显然，这糟透了。糟得不能再糟。你可以漂回塔夫罗斯，但靠推进器的速度，得耗上好几周，而维修费远不是你之前工作攒下的那点可怜积蓄能应付的。一定还有别的办法！

original: A quick scan of nearby space fills your heart with a moment’s hope: not far from your current position, thirty minutes away, there’s an old mining installation still broadcasting its landing codes - Steele Tech codes! It must be an old derelict, a relic of your dad’s company first prospecting the system centuries ago. If it’s that old, it might just have the parts you’re looking for. And if not, surely there’ll be an emergency shuttle or something you can take the rest of the way to Mhen’ga!
translation: 快速扫描一下附近的空间，你的心中顿时充满了希望：在离你当前位置不远的地方，30 分钟航程之外，有一个古老的采矿装置仍在播放着陆代码——斯蒂尔科技代码！它一定是个废弃的老古董，是你爸爸的公司几个世纪前第一次勘探这个系统时留下的遗迹。如果它真的那么老，也许就有你要找的零件。如果没有，肯定会有紧急穿梭机之类的东西，你可以走完去姆恩加星的路！

original: At this point there’s no harm in exploring, right? Taking a deep breath, you settle back into the captain’s chair and engage your thrusters, guiding the old workhorse down towards the station. From the outside it looks like just a particularly large black-brown hunk of floating space rock, but as you get nearer, you can see external metal struts connecting different levels of the station inside, and a flickering blue bubble-shield protecting the landing bays. You choose one at random and ease through it, feeling your hair stand on end as the outdated containment screen parts around your ship, charging the hull with energy.
translation: 此时此刻，探索一下也无妨，不是吗？深吸一口气，你坐回船长椅，启动推进器，引导老式飞船向空间站驶去。从远处看，它只是一块特别大的黑褐色浮空岩石，但当你靠近时，你可以看到外部的金属支柱连接着空间站内部的不同楼层，还有一个闪烁的蓝色气泡防护罩保护着着陆舱。你随意选择了一个，然后轻松地穿过它，当过时的防护屏围绕着你的飞船，为船体注入能量时，你会感到汗毛竖起。

original: But you’re here, safe and sound as you can be. Now to find a replacement plasma conduit so you can get the hell out of here! You tap at your ship’s systems, using the Casstech’s old array of ancient Steele Tech codes to connect to the station’s computers and pull up an inventory manifest. You’re in luck! There’s a spare! The logs list it as stored in a custodial closet onboard, one buried deep in the station’s guts - just beyond life support.
translation: 但你在这里，安然无恙。现在要找一个替代等离子导管，这样你就可以离开这个鬼地方了！你打开飞船的系统，用卡斯泰克的古老斯蒂尔科技密码阵列连接到空间站的电脑，调出一份清单。你运气不错！有一个备用的！日志显示，它被存放在船上的一个保管柜里，一个深埋在空间站内部的柜子里——只是在生命支持系统之外。

original: (Select “Exit” to disembark from your ship. You can do this any time your ship is parked at a safe location.)
translation: (选择 退出 即可下船。你可以在飞船停靠在安全地点后随时下船。）

original: Good thing Victor’s scientists made you to be the perfect heir. So long as you aren’t distracted by your ability to experience inhuman amounts of pleasure, you’ll do just fine! ...Now, what to do with all this silicone?
You were born a bit different than expected, but from differences come strength... and a very productive set of genitals.
Despite a curious blend of canine and shark genes finding their way into your genome, you are still Victor Steele’s heir - but one with two big-ass dog-dicks and a desire to use them.
You’ve always been ridiculously fertile. The doctors warned you at a young age. Just a glance at the wrong alien and you’ll blow up with babies! But that’s not new.
You’ve always been a big-dicked, dragon-like hermaphrodite.
Good thing Victor’s scientists made you to be the perfect heir. So long as you aren’t distracted by your ability to experience inhuman amounts of pleasure, you’ll do just fine!
You always had breasts almost as large as you are intelligent!
You’ve always had an amazing physique, and the best of both genders - the better to colonize new worlds with your young.
You’ve always been a bit of a monkey. Nothing wrong with that.
You’ve always been a bit of an aquatic terror, haven’t you?
You’ve always been a sexy goo-girl!
You’ve always been a draconic hermaphrodite with lots of neon-green accents!
You’ve always been a tall husky girl, just waiting to play!
You’ve always been an exceptionally endowed dragoness.
You’ve always been a neuter. Life’s easier that way.

None of the doctors or scientists could ever figure out what went ‘wrong’ with you, it was as if some supernatural force meddled with you. Nor could they ever explain the your ‘past life’ memories, but you’re certain you’ve always been this way.
Reality seems to warp, but then, things are as they always have been, aren’t they? You look over your [pc.chest], [pc.crotch]. Yup, everything is fine.
translation: 现实似乎扭曲了一瞬，但随即又恢复如常，不是吗？你低头看看自己的[pc.chest]和[pc.crotch]。嗯，一切安好。

original: The Complete the Circuit minigame is a game where you must guide a beam from an emitter to a goal block to complete the circuit system. It is possible to have multiple emitters, beams and goals. The main objective is to have all emitters and goals connected without breaking a beam or landing a beam into a trap.
This is an example of a 9 x 5 layout which includes many possible tiles that may be encountered.
Emitters are the starting points for the circuit system. Each emitter will project a beam in the direction if its openings.
Goals are the end points for the circuit system. Each goal requires all its openings connected by beams and when all goals are connected, the circuit is completed.
Static blocks include various configurations: Solid, Free, Cap, Coupling, Elbow and Tee.
A Solid block will prevent any beams from passing through it, whereas a Free Space will allow any beams to pass straight through, even allowing other beams to cross.

A Coupling block allows a beam to pass through in a straight line. An Elbow block will redirect the beam to a different direction. A Tee block can split a beam into two or converge two beams into one.
A Cap block serves more as a dead end.
Dynamic blocks are similar to Static blocks but with the ability to be manipulated, changing the route for oncoming beams.
Interacting with a Dynamic block will rotate it clockwise 90 degrees with each click. Using the dynamic blocks is essential to solving the puzzle and completing the circuit.
Each Teleporter has one possible entrance/exit.
Teleporters will take in a beam and move it from one teleporter to another of the same port number.
Beware, this kind of block is rigged with a trap.
If a beam makes contact with a trap, it will result in a system failure.
Override blocks have a special ability and can have more than one opening.
If a beam enters an Override block, all specially-marked Static blocks on the board will convert into Dynamic blocks.
This is the solved version of the 9 x 5 example.
